title: parcel-bundle/v1
description: Canonical parcel reference bundle emitted by SITE-RECON-001 and consumed by downstream RE workers (TITLE-ABSTRACT-001, ZONING-001, LAW-LANDUSE-001, W-002 Real Estate Analyst, FEASIBILITY-001). Carries the minimum identification + audit lineage a downstream worker needs to pull deeper data without re-discovering the parcel.
inheritsFrom: CODEX S52.43 (Platform RAAS Invariants — Reagan Rule + Britney Rule + EH-01)
CODEX S52.15 (Audit Trail Architecture — DTC + Logbook)

parcelId.description: Canonical identification. APN is the legal anchor; attomId is the ATTOM lookup key; dtcId is the SOCIII Vault DTC reference.

apn.description: Assessor Parcel Number as recorded by the county. Verbatim — no normalization that loses leading zeros.

attomId.description: ATTOM property record ID. Optional but required for downstream ATTOM pulls.

dtcId.description: SOCIII Vault DTC reference if a parcel-DTC has been minted; absent if this is a fresh discovery.

fips.description: 5-digit county FIPS code (state+county). Required for HUD / Census ACS lookups.

verifyMethod.description: How the lat/lng + address were verified. user_supplied_unverified per Reagan Rule (CODEX S52.43 §5) — accepted graciously, treated as unverified until cross-referenced.

jurisdiction.description: Layered jurisdiction stack for the parcel. Each layer carries the rule code + a live retrievalUrl (Britney Rule + EH-01 — no model-recalled URLs).

municipality.description: City / town / village. Absent for unincorporated parcels.

physical.description: Physical parcel characteristics. All numeric fields carry units; never bare numbers.

ownership.description: Current owner of record. NEVER inferred — must come from authoritative source (county recorder OR ATTOM) with retrievedAt timestamp.

verified.description: Per Reagan Rule — true only if cross-referenced; false default for single-source pulls.

rights.description: Property rights stratum. In the US, especially the Western states, surface ownership is often severed from the rights ABOVE and BELOW the surface. A title report that only covers surface is structurally blind. Each stratum is independently ownable, leaseable, and litigable. Default for any stratum: 'unknown' — gap-declare per EH-04 rather than silently assume it stays with surface.

surface.description: Surface estate — the right to occupy and use the surface of the land. The default 'parcel' in most consumer mental models.

mineral.description: Subsurface mineral rights — oil / gas / coal / hardrock / sand-and-gravel. In Sublette WY, Mountain West, OK, TX, and most of the West, minerals are commonly SEVERED from surface and held by separate parties or the federal government (BLM). Severance is the rule, not the exception. Coal-bed methane (CBM), produced water, and helium are sub-strata under mineral rights with their own histories.

severed.description: True if mineral estate is held separately from surface. CRITICAL field for Western US analysis.

leaseHolder.description: Active lessee (operator) if minerals are leased — common in oil/gas country.

water.description: Water rights. In Western prior-appropriation states (WY, CO, MT, ID, UT, NV, AZ, NM + parts of CA, OR, WA), water rights are PROPERTY independent of land — 'first in time, first in right'. A parcel can have surface rights without any water right; a water right can be severed and sold separately. In riparian-doctrine states (East / Midwest) water rights generally run with land but are not unlimited. Federal reserved rights (Winters doctrine) overlay both.

priorityDate.description: Critical in prior-appropriation states — earlier date = senior right.

air.description: Air rights — the column of airspace above the parcel up to navigable airspace (FAA-defined, generally 500 ft AGL in uncongested areas, but lower under approach/departure cones). NYC Transferable Development Rights (TDR) market is the canonical commercial example; rooftop solar leases, billboard rights, helipad easements all live here. Below 500 ft AGL is parcel-owner space; above is federal airspace.

tdrAvailable.description: Transferable Development Rights — sellable unused FAR. NYC, San Francisco, Chicago, Seattle, etc.

faaProximity.description: Distance to nearest airport / heliport / Class B/C/D airspace. Affects build height + drone operations.

spectrum.description: Radio spectrum / telecom rights. Cell tower leases, microwave repeater easements, broadcast easements all create a separate rights stratum. FCC licenses are geographic-bound; a parcel that hosts a tower carries a long-term lease that affects valuation + transfer rights. Rural areas + ridgelines + tall buildings are the load-bearing examples.

digital.description: Digital / data rights attached to the parcel. Emerging stratum — includes: data center colocation rights, fiber easements, IoT-sensor data ownership, geo-fenced licensed-content rights, blockchain-anchored digital twin pointers. Not yet recognized in most county recorder systems but increasingly material — a parcel hosting a Bitcoin mining operation, an EV charging hub, or a cloud data center carries digital-rights structure that affects valuation + transfer. SOCIII's own audit anchoring (PLAT-008) is a digital-rights instance attached to the parcel DTC.

ioTSensorOwnership.description: Who owns data emitted by sensors on the parcel (smart meters, soil monitors, surveillance, etc.).

platformAnchors.description: SOCIII-platform anchors attached to this parcel — DTC compound entries, logbook references. The parcel's own audit-chain rights stratum.

verificationStatus.description: Audit lineage. Required for the bundle to be honored by downstream workers — without verification metadata a downstream cannot anchor its own outputs.

confidence.description: RAG verdict. GREEN: all required fields verified against ≥2 sources OR 1 authoritative source. YELLOW: required fields present, single-source. RED: required fields missing or contradictory across sources.

verifiedFields.description: Dot-paths of fields that have been cross-referenced. E.g., 'ownership.currentOwner', 'physical.lotSizeSqFt'.

unverifiedFields.description: Dot-paths of fields present but NOT cross-referenced. Downstream workers must surface a 'based on unverified data' caveat when relying on these.

missingFields.description: Required-or-expected fields the source did not provide. Per EH-04 hyper-local gap declaration.

auditAnchor.description: PLAT-008 anchor reference if the parcel-bundle has been recorded to a DTC logbook. Optional; required when the bundle flows downstream to a worker that itself anchors.

media.description: Optional video-tile/v1 attachments — drone aerials, council hearings, news clips contextually tied to the parcel.

activePersonaId.description: Per Active Persona Gate (CODEX S52.43 §3) — who paid for the discovery.

notes.reagan_rule_application: Any field populated from user_supplied input MUST carry source: 'user_supplied' + verified: false until cross-referenced against ATTOM or county recorder. Downstream workers treat unverified fields as advisory, not authoritative.
notes.britney_rule_application: Downstream workers MUST NOT add detail to a parcel-bundle field that the bundle didn't carry. If TITLE-ABSTRACT-001 needs an APN-suffix detail not in the bundle, it pulls it itself — never invents it.
notes.deposition_rule_application: When auditAnchor is populated, the bundle's full retrievedHash chain is reconstructable from the logbook entry. A forensic auditor three years later can verify which fields were known at discovery time vs added by downstream workers.
notes.accepts_contract_application: Workers declaring 'accepts: ["parcel-bundle/v1"]' in their catalog gain a typed input contract. The catalog discovery layer (CODEX S52.42) uses this to route handoffs dynamically without hardcoded target lists.
